text: R/import-standalone-backends.R edited . standalone file vendored ACCIDDA/flexstanr — single source truth portable backend layer shared across imuGAP, hestia, SeverityEstimate (see #112). header says “edit hand.” change backend behavior, edit flexstanr, re-sync . preferred way justfile recipe: re-runs use_standalone vendored source restores DESCRIPTION afterwards. manual equivalent : note call also rewrites DESCRIPTION — strips version pins, e.g. rstan (>= 2.18.1) → rstan (usethis#2198) — must revert DESCRIPTION change hand; re-sync touch R/import-standalone-backends.R. recipe revert . vendored header’s # repo: ACCIDDA/flexstanr line parses R, commented_code_linter disabled globally .lintr (rather excluding file); vendored file otherwise linted like . use_standalone one-shot copy, vendored file can fall behind upstream. backends-sync workflow (weekly) discovers every vendored standalone file, reads one’s source # Source: header, compares upstream, — anything drifted — opens pull request refreshed copies. title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text:
name imuGAP stands “Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection”. package allows user synthesize across multiple data sources make predictions vaccination coverage user-defined populations interest. example, one use package : Estimate current vaccination coverage location across different age groups Estimate coverage (uptake) given birth cohort (e.g. people born 1990) across life course (.e. age birth current age) Fill gaps observed coverage data (e.g. school doesn’t report vaccination coverage certain year) specifically, package provides stan-based model estimating vaccination coverage location, cohort, age childhood infectious diseases, measles. core model represents target population life-long propensity vaccination; proportion, ϕ\phi, population unlikely vaccinate complementary proportion, 1−ϕ1 - \phi, likely vaccinate. population experiences vaccination rate, λ\lambda, model time eras, according vaccination eligibility schedule, ν
u. core parameters can vary time location, user-specifiable way. Focusing just core model element, imagine particular population location ii cohort aa (aa denotes start time period group born). cohort now age tt, vaccine schedule first dose ν(t)
u(t), expected fraction group least one dose : P(≥1 dose)=(1−ϕi,)(1−exp{−∫atλi,(s)ν(s)ds}) P(\ge	extrm{1 dose}) = \left(1 - \phi_{, }ight) \left(1 - \exp\left\{-\int_a^{t} \lambda_{, }(s)
u(s) d	extrm{s}ight\}ight) say, representing vaccination coverage via survival-like model. model generalizes approach first dose arbitrary sequential dose coverage, subsequent dose conditional previous dose receipt.

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: walkthrough demonstrates workflow fitting model predicting coverage simulated data. package includes several bundled datasets demonstration, representing nested geographic hierarchy (State -> Counties -> Schools) population uptake two dose vaccine, like MMR measles.

what: 1. Preparing and Validating the Input Data
title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection

However, shown next section, sampling() method also automatically canonicalizes inputs.

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: ensure data integrity, canonicalize_* functions enforce strict rules input data format constraints. example, modify observations data number positive cases exceeds total sample size sample_n, validation function raise clear error: Similarly, locations data contains duplicate location IDs, canonicalize_locations() detect duplication throw error: See canonicalize_* function documentation complete validation requirements.
code: # Create a copy with an invalid observation (positive > sample_n) invalid_obs <- copy(observations_sim[, .(obs_id, loc_id, positive, sample_n, censored)]) invalid_obs[1, positive := sample_n + 10]  # This will fail validation and throw an error: tryCatch(   canonicalize_observations(invalid_obs),   error = function(e) message("Caught expected error: ", e$message) ) #> Caught expected error: positive must be <= sample_n; found 1 invalid observations with offending ids: 1 # Create a copy with a duplicate location ID invalid_locs <- rbind(   locations_sim,   data.frame(loc_id = "Scruggs", parent_id = "State") )  # This will fail validation: tryCatch(   canonicalize_locations(invalid_locs),   error = function(e) message("Caught expected error: ", e$message) ) #> Caught expected error: locations$loc_id must be unique; found 1 duplicates: 29

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: Using prepared input datasets, can fit Bayesian model using sampling(). options sampler can configured using stan_options(). compiling Stan model running MCMC chain can take time, show code without executing . walkthrough, load pre-computed fit object fit_sim bundled package: model fit, can extract posterior draws model parameters using extract_imugap(). example, let’s extract B-spline coefficients representing state-level vaccine uptake baseline: can also look trace plots selected parameters ensure convergence reached.
code: fit_sim <- sampling(   observations_sim, populations_sim, locations_sim,   stan_opts = stan_options(     iter = 2000, chains = 4, refresh = 0, seed = 1L   ) ) data("fit_sim", package = "imuGAP") beta_draws <- extract_imugap(fit_sim, pars = "beta_bs") str(beta_draws) #> List of 1 #>  $ beta_bs: num [1:2000, 1:5] -1.96 -1.66 -1.78 -1.86 -1.8 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. ..$ iterations: NULL #>   .. ..$           : NULL bayesplot::mcmc_trace(fit_sim$stanfit,   pars = c(     "beta_bs[1]", "beta_bs[2]", "beta_bs[3]",     "beta_bs[4]", "beta_bs[5]"   ) ) + theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = c(.9, .1), legend.justification = c(1, 0))

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: predict vaccine coverage target population (can include locations cohorts without direct observations, long exist locations hierarchy), first define target grid using create_target(). Note predictions can made birth cohorts locations least observations included estimation run. words, model predict coverage future birth cohorts unobserved locations. example, can generate “snapshot” prediction target locations, including State County levels, across ages 1 18:
code: target_sim <- create_target(   location = unique(locations_sim$loc_id), age = 1:18,   cohort = max(populations_sim$cohort) - 18, dose = c(1, 2), mode = "snapshot" ) head(target_sim) #>    obs_c_id               loc_id   age cohort  dose weight #>       <int>               <char> <int>  <num> <num>  <num> #> 1:        1                State     1     30     1      1 #> 2:        2              Scruggs     1     30     1      1 #> 3:        3               Simone     1     30     1      1 #> 4:        4               Watson     1     30     1      1 #> 5:        5 Chickadee Elementary     1     30     1      1 #> 6:        6     Nuthatch Academy     1     30     1      1

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: Finally, run predict() generate predicted coverage probabilities target population combination. default uses every posterior draw; pass posterior_size predict smaller sub-sample taken end chain. Generating predictions also runs Stan model (generated quantities mode) can time-consuming, show code without executing : Instead, load pre-computed prediction results predict_sim bundled package. object class imugap_predict contains 3D draws array (predict_sim$draws) MCMC draws prediction target well target information (predict_sim$target). can summarize predictions get posterior mean credible intervals across target location, age, doses requested: Now let’s visualize results. First take look overall state coverage cohort. Note lower coverage among 5 year olds due eligible second dose one year.  can also look trend coverage age county level. Note follow trend state differing magnitude.  Next, can look distribution school-level coverage estimates age group. Let’s focus Scruggs County example.  Finally let’s look selected schools see predicted coverage compared true underlying coverage data simulation process.
code: predict_sim <- predict(object = fit_sim, target = target_sim, posterior_size = 100) data("predict_sim", package = "imuGAP") # Calculate the posterior mean coverage probability for each location and dose at age 5 summary_predict <- summary(predict_sim) head(summary_predict) #>    obs_c_id               loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id obs_id #>       <int>               <char> <int>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int>  <int> #> 1:        1                State     1     30     1      1        1      1 #> 2:        2              Scruggs     1     30     1      1        2      2 #> 3:        3               Simone     1     30     1      1        3      3 #> 4:        4               Watson     1     30     1      1        4      4 #> 5:        5 Chickadee Elementary     1     30     1      1        8      5 #> 6:        6     Nuthatch Academy     1     30     1      1       11      6 #>     mean  q2_5   q50 q97_5 #>    <num> <num> <num> <num> #> 1:     0     0     0     0 #> 2:     0     0     0     0 #> 3:     0     0     0     0 #> 4:     0     0     0     0 #> 5:     0     0     0     0 #> 6:     0     0     0     0 summary_predict |>   subset(loc_id == "State" & dose == 2 & age > 4) |>   ggplot() +   aes(x = age) +   geom_line(aes(y = q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q2_5, ymax = q97_5), alpha = 0.5) +   theme_bw() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 5:18, minor_breaks = NULL) +   labs(x = "Age", y = "Coverage", title = "State-level two dose coverage") summary_predict |>   subset(loc_id %in% c("Scruggs", "Simone", "Watson") & dose == 2 & age > 4) |>   ggplot() +   aes(x = age) +   geom_line(aes(y = q50, color = loc_id)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q2_5, ymax = q97_5, fill = loc_id), alpha = 0.2) +   theme_bw() +   theme(     legend.position = "inside", legend.position.inside = c(.2, 0.05),     legend.justification.inside = c(0, 0)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 5:18, minor_breaks = NULL) +   scale_color_discrete(NULL, aesthetics = c("color", "fill")) +   labs(     x = "Age", y = "Coverage", title = "County-level two dose coverage"   ) scruggs_schools <- locations_sim[parent_id == "Scruggs", loc_id] summary_predict |>   subset(     loc_id %in% scruggs_schools & dose == 2 & age > 4   ) |>   ggplot() +   geom_boxplot(aes(x = factor(age), y = q50)) +   theme_bw() +   labs(     x = "Age", y = "Coverage",     title = "Distribution of School-Level Coverage For Scruggs County"   ) schools <- c(   "Towhee Children's Academy", # ~380 per grade   "Flycatcher Elementary", # ~110 per grade   "Sparrow School" # ~60 per grade )  # Subset to targets of interest (all retained posterior draws) predict_sub <- predict_sim |>   subset(loc_id %in% schools & dose == 2 & age > 4)  # Get the pre-computed background coverage matching the subsetted target latent_ref <- copy(predict_sub$target) latent_ref$coverage <- latent_params_sim$coverage[predict_sub$target$obs_id]   # Convert predictions to a long-format data.frame draws_df <- as.data.frame(predict_sub)  # Now plot it all ggplot() +   aes(age, coverage, color = loc_id) +   geom_point(     data = draws_df,     alpha = 1 / 256, shape = 16,     position = position_jitterdodge(       dodge.width = 0.5,       jitter.width = 0.15     )   ) +   geom_point(     data = latent_ref,     mapping = aes(shape = "True value"),     fill = NA   ) +   theme_bw() +   scale_shape_manual(     name = "",     values = c("True value" = 24)   ) +   scale_color_discrete(NULL, aesthetics = c("color", "fill")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 5:18, minor_breaks = NULL) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   labs(color = "School", x = "Age", y = "Coverage")

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize

code: canonicalize_locations(locations)  canonicalize_observations(observations, drop_extra = TRUE)  canonicalize_populations(   populations,   observations,   locations,   max_cohort,   max_age,   max_dose = 2L )

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: locations [data.frame()], columns loc_id parent_id, type. See Details restrictions. observations [data.frame()], observed data, least three columns: obs_id column; type, long unique, non-NA positive column; non-negative integers, observed number vaccinated individuals sample_n column; positive integers, number individuals sampled, must greater equal "positive" optionally, censored column; numeric, NA (uncensored) 1 (right-censored); present, assumed NA drop_extra logical scalar; drop extraneous columns? (default: yes) populations [data.frame()], observation meta data, columns obs_id, type; observation row concerns (.e. id shared observations data object) loc_id, type; location row concerns (.e. id shared locations data object) dose, non-zero, positive integer (1, 2, ...); dose row concerns cohort, positive integer; cohort location row concerns age, positive integer; age cohort row concerns weight, numeric, (0, 1); relative contribution row observation. Optional population row unique obs_id. max_cohort present, maximum cohort present? max_age present, maximum age present? max_dose maximum dose number allow (default: 2L)

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: canonicalize_locations returns data.table, : loc_id, parent_id columns originally supplied, possibly reordered loc_c_id, loc_cp_id columns, canonicalized id/parent_id columns, representing order used sampler layer column, integer 1 (root), 2 (root children), 3 (grandchildren), &c layer_bound column, integer starting 1 layer. provides index slice information used stan model. canonicalize_observations returns canonical observation object, [data.table()] : obs_c_id column, integer sequence 1; order observations passed estimation original obs_id column, possibly reordered positive sample_n columns, possibly reordered "censored" column; NA, present original observations argument canonicalize_populations returns canonical populations object, mirroring input populations, following updates: obs_c_id, observation id row concerns, canonicalized match canonical observation ids loc_c_id, location id row concerns, canonicalized match reordered obs_c_id order

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: imuGAP hierarchical modeling framework requires data structures adhere specific relational format constraints. three canonicalize functions process validate inputs described :

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: [sampling()] sampler works hierarchical model locations, must provided structure. method checks location structure validity, returns canonical version including layer membership. valid structure : unique root, cycles, duplicate loc_ids Users may explicitly identify root loc_id providing row parent_id equal NA. Otherwise, parent_id appear loc_id treated root. input valid, method create canonicalized version. version, ids run 1:N, N number distinct ids. order determined layer order, position parent within layer, "natural" order (.e., whatever base R sort() yields).

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: observations object documents observations used fit model. Conceptually, row represents observation vaccination status within population. population need uniform (see [canonicalize_populations()]) concerning single cohort time: observation generally best available resolution data. resolution can vary across rows. sampler uses information resolutions automatically figure compare latent process model different observations. optional censored column: model supports vaccination status indicators vaccine specific well represent individual set vaccines (including target vaccine). specific coverage target vaccine right-censored latter case: full-set-coverage minimum coverage target. least data censored, must supply censored column correctly estimate coverage. Mark uncensored observations NA, right-censored observations 1. Note 0 valid value time; preserving potential future support left-censoring.

code: create_target(   location,   age,   cohort,   dose,   mode = c("error", "enumerate", "recycle", "snapshot") )

title: Construct a target grid for prediction — create_target
text: location vector location IDs target. age vector ages predict coverage, consistent [canonicalize_populations()]. cohort vector cohorts predict coverage, consistent [canonicalize_populations()]. dose vector doses predict coverage, consistent [canonicalize_observations()]. mode one "error" (default), "enumerate", "recycle", "snapshot", controlling vector inputs combine: "error": vector inputs must length. "enumerate": combinations inputs. "recycle": recycle inputs least-common-multiple length. "snapshot": cohort must single reference value (oldest cohort); locations, ages, doses enumerated cohort age age + cohort constant, using maximum value age set constant (cohort_i = cohort_ref + max(age) - age_i), .e. snapshot time.

title: imuGAP 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-22 First public release. Initial feature set: sampling(): fits imuGAP Bayesian hierarchical coverage model via rstan::sampling() returns imugap_fit object wrapping underlying stanfit together model settings dataset metadata. predict.imugap_fit(): posterior-predicts coverage probabilities user-supplied target population grid using rstan::gqs(). extract_imugap(): convenience wrapper around rstan::extract() pulling common imuGAP parameters (defaults state-level B-spline coefficients beta_bs). canonicalize_locations(), canonicalize_observations(), canonicalize_populations(): validate convert user-supplied data canonical forms required sampler. imugap_options(): configures model-side settings (B-spline degrees freedom, dose schedule, model object). stan_options(): configures Stan sampler settings (iter, chains, seed, etc.) input validation. impute_school_coverage_process_v6 (adds county- school-level random effects; current default). Bundled example datasets end--end examples tests: locations_sim, observations_sim, populations_sim, latent_params_sim, predict_sim, target_sim, reference fit_sim stanfit fixture. pkgdown documentation site published https://accidda.github.io/imuGAP/.
